# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI CD workflow with GitHub Actions
on: [push] # or [pull_request] if you want this action on PRs only  (optional)  
jobs:   
  build-and-test:    
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest     
    steps:        
       - name: Checkout code       
         uses: actions/checkout@v2 # or use community/actions-checkout if you prefer not to pin the version (optional)  
          with: 
            fetch-depth: 0     # Shallow clones should be disabled for a better relevancy of analysis     
       - name: Set up Python       
         uses: actions/setup-python@v2   
       - name: Install dependencies from requirements.txt  
         run: |         
           python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip     # Upgrade pip to latest version (optional) 
           if [ -f requirements.txt ] ; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi     
        env:      
            PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: "1"   # Disable creation of bytecode-optimized .pyc files, which can slow down development (optional) 
         
       - name: Run tests   
         run: |    
           pytest --cov=.      # Or your test command if needed
